executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware wave with 100+ Canadian organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart with a confirmed database leak, Peterborough Public Health, and the Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group alone has claimed 18 Canadian victims, followed by Play (9 victims), Clop (14 victims), and SafePay (11 victims). Critical infrastructure including healthcare, education, public sector, and financial services has been severely impacted, representing a national-scale cyber crisis requiring immediate coordinated response.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 18 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra (law firm), Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG CPA, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a catastrophic ransomware epidemic with over 100 organizations compromised in 48 hours across every province and sector. Critical sectors hit include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services, Bison Family Medical), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Collège Supérieur De Montréal), public sector (Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Thunder Bay Counselling), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose hit by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) breached by Sinobi, Kids & Company (childcare services) breached by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered confirmed database leak via Everest ransomware group, Morison Insurance Brokers breached by Sinobi, multiple accounting/CPA firms compromised (Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, Bergmanis Preyra law firm)

report_2w.executive_summary: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197, a critical remote code execution vulnerability discovered by an AI researcher, is now under active exploitation and has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Fortinet FortiClientEMS CVE-2026-35616 is the subject of an official Canadian government (CCCS) advisory. Cisco patched four critical vulnerabilities in Identity Services Engine and Webex enabling remote code execution. Amtrak suffered a data breach exposing 2.1 million accounts, allegedly by threat actor ShinyHunters. No direct impacts to major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.

items.description: Remote code execution vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ discovered by Anthropic AI researcher
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Fortinet FortiClientEMS - subject of Canadian CCCS advisory
items.cia_impact: Unknown - details not specified in advisory

items.description: Vulnerability in end-of-life TP-Link Wi-Fi devices seeing active exploitation attempts
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: 17-year-old Microsoft Excel remote code execution vulnerability mentioned in threat roundup
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: Medium, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

items.description: Critical vulnerability with CVSS 10.0 score mentioned in Dutch threat radar
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.id: Cisco ISE and Webex Critical RCE
items.description: Four critical vulnerabilities in Cisco Identity Services Engine and Webex enabling remote code execution
items.software_affected: Cisco Identity Services Engine, Cisco Webex
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High
items.access_required: Network / Authentication varies by specific CVE

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Amtrak - exposed information of 2.1 million accounts
items.ttps: T1589.001 (Gather Victim Identity Information), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), likely credential theft and data exfiltration

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: Gruppo ICM SPA (unverified claim)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact - ransomware), T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel)

items.name: Lazarus (APT38)
items.targets: General targeting via fake job invitations and dream job lures
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain - NPM packages)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: Medicalnetworks CJ GmbH & Co. KG
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact - ransomware), T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel)

items.name: Iranian threat actors
items.targets: Israeli water systems via ZionSiphon malware targeting ICS protocols
items.ttps: T1091 (Replication Through Removable Media - USB propagation), T1595.002 (Active Scanning: Vulnerability Scanning of ICS protocols), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

items.name: Vidar Stealer operators
items.targets: General credential and information theft campaigns
items.ttps: T1555 (Credentials from Password Stores), T1005 (Data from Local System), rising trend with 16 new samples and 100 C2 servers observed

items.name: Agent Tesla operators
items.targets: General credential theft and keylogging campaigns
items.ttps: T1056.001 (Input Capture: Keylogging), T1555 (Credentials from Password Stores), stable trend with 23 new samples

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Advisory AL26-007 regarding CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS. No breaches of major Canadian retailers (Loblaws, Canadian Tire, Shoppers Drug Mart) or financial institutions (RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO, CIBC) were identified in this reporting period. General Canadian business and political news was present but no cybersecurity incidents specific to critical Canadian infrastructure.
